title: Hissing Microphone
description:
A known problem with some microphones is the “hissing s”. That is, sometimes the sound of the letter s is particularly pronounced; it stands out from the rest of the word in an unpleasant way.

Of particular annoyance are words that contain the letter s twice in a row. Words like amiss, kiss, mississippi and even hiss itself.
input: The input contains a single string on a single line. This string consists of only lowercase letters (no spaces) and has between 1 and 30 characters.
output: Output a single line. If the input string contains two consecutive occurrences of the letter s, then output hiss. Otherwise, output no hiss.
source: , ICPC, Regionals, North America, Rocky Mountain Regional, 2017 Rocky Mountain Regional Contest
